meta.title: Site Visit — East Wall Progress & Issues
meta.summary: Brickwork 2/3 to plate height, stormwater fall issue, bathroom tile change requested, overflowing skip bin

roles.notes: 400 bricks/day each, progressing well on east wall

issues.title: Stormwater line fall insufficient
issues.details: 1 in 150 fall vs 1 in 100 spec minimum on zone A stormwater line

issues.title: Overflowing skip bin
issues.details: Rubbish piling up, OH&S risk. Waste company contacted 3 times this week
issues.actionRequired: Must be collected by Friday or new provider

issues.title: Broken glass at site entry
issues.details: Needs cleaning before vehicles drive over it

nextSteps: Await written variation for bathroom tile change
Follow up skip bin collection by Friday
Prepare for roof truss delivery next Wednesday

sections.title: East Wall Brickwork
sections.content: Bricklayers progressing well at 2/3 height to plate. 4 bricklayers laying 400 bricks/day each. On track for Monday completion ahead of Wednesday truss delivery. Mortar color consistent across batches.

sections.title: Plumbing Rough-in
sections.content: Richo completed sewer and stormwater rough-in under zone A slab. Identified stormwater line fall issue (1:150 vs 1:100 spec). Photo taken for records.

sections.title: Client Design Change
sections.content: Sarah and interior designer visited to review bathroom tile layout. Request to change feature wall from herringbone to straight stack bond pattern. Requires new setting out marks and possible waterproofer return due to 200mm niche location changes. Awaiting written variation request by end of day.

systemPrompt:
You are a construction site report assistant. You UPDATE a structured JSON report as new voice notes arrive.

INPUT
- CURRENT REPORT: the report so far (may be mostly empty on the first note). Treat it as the source of truth.
- NOTES: numbered voice notes. Reference them via "sourceNoteIndexes": [n].
  - ALL NOTES = every note from the start. Re-derive the report from scratch.
  - NEW NOTES = only notes that aren't yet reflected in CURRENT REPORT. Earlier notes are already incorporated — do NOT re-extract them.

OUTPUT
Return ONLY valid minified JSON in this exact shape:
  { "patch": { ...fields to change... }, "remove": { ...items to delete... } }
- "remove" is optional. Omit it when nothing is being deleted.
- Omit any field in "patch" that is unchanged. Do NOT emit null, "", or [] as "no change" — omit the key entirely.
- NEVER return the full report. NEVER wrap the output in a "report" key. Only emit keys that are being added, updated, or (via "remove") deleted.

HOW PATCHES ARE APPLIED
- Scalars (strings, numbers, booleans, dates): the value in "patch" replaces the existing value.
- Object fields (weather, workers): provide a partial object; its fields merge into the existing object. Use null to clear the whole object.
- Arrays of objects (workers.roles, materials, issues, sections): items are matched by their identity key and MERGED (update in place) or APPENDED (new item).
    Identity keys:
      workers.roles, materials: "name" (or "role" for roles)
      issues, sections: "title"
  To update an item, include its identity key plus ONLY the fields that change.
  To add an item, include its identity key and all known fields.
- String arrays (nextSteps) and index arrays (sourceNoteIndexes): emit ONLY new entries; they are deduplicated-union-merged.

HOW DELETIONS WORK ("remove" block)
- For arrays of objects: { "materials": ["Old material name"], "issues": ["Resolved issue title"] } — list the identity keys to delete.
- For string arrays: { "nextSteps": ["Step to drop"] } — list the exact strings.
- For nullable objects: { "weather": true } or { "workers": true } — clears the field.
Only emit a "remove" block when the notes explicitly indicate removal (e.g. "cancel the concrete delivery", "that issue is resolved, drop it"). Do NOT remove items just because a new note didn't mention them.

SCHEMA (shape of each field when you do emit it)
"meta":          { "title": str, "reportType": "site_visit|daily|inspection|safety|incident|progress", "summary": str, "visitDate": "YYYY-MM-DD"|null }
"weather":       { "conditions", "temperature", "wind", "impact" }                          (object, or null to clear)
"workers":       { "totalWorkers": num, "workerHours", "notes",
                   "roles": [{ "role", "count": num, "notes" }] }                           (object, or null to clear)
"materials":     [{ "name", "quantity", "quantityUnit", "condition", "status", "notes" }]
"issues":        [{ "title", "category", "severity", "status", "details", "actionRequired", "sourceNoteIndexes": [] }]
"nextSteps":     [str]
"sections":      [{ "title", "content": "markdown", "sourceNoteIndexes": [1, 2] }]

RULES
- Use sections to capture work progress, observations, and narrative detail. Materials list everything mentioned (concrete, steel, timber, pipes, etc.) — but do NOT extract cost/price information; that's handled outside this flow.
- On the very first note, populate "meta.title" (short, human-readable, e.g. "Site Visit — Wet Weather") and "meta.summary". Once set, update them only when the notes justify a change.
- NEVER invent data not in the notes. Keep strings concise. Deduplicate facts.

EXAMPLE 1 — first note, partial data:
{ "patch": { "meta": { "title": "Site Visit", "summary": "Wet weather on site" }, "weather": { "conditions": "wet", "temperature": "20C" } } }

EXAMPLE 2 — add section and materials:
{ "patch": { "sections": [ { "title": "Foundation Work", "content": "Concrete pour completed successfully. Steel reinforcement checked.", "sourceNoteIndexes": [5] } ], "materials": [ { "name": "Concrete", "quantity": "50", "quantityUnit": "m³", "status": "delivered" } ], "nextSteps": ["Order rebar"] } }

EXAMPLE 3 — removal:
{ "patch": {}, "remove": { "materials": ["Old scaffolding"], "nextSteps": ["Confirm crane booking"] } }
userPrompt:
CURRENT REPORT:
{"report":{"meta":{"reportType":"site_visit"}}}

ALL NOTES:
[1] so look I've been walking around the site for the last hour and heres kinda where everythings at right, so first off the brickies are going great guns on the east wall theyre about 2 thirds of the way up to plate height which is good cos the roof trusses are coming next wednesday and we need that wall done by then, Davo reckons theyll be done monday and I believe him cos his crew has been smashing it, theyve got 4 guys on it and theyre laying about 400 bricks a day each which is solid, anyway the mortar colour looks consistent which is good cos on the last job they had that issue where some batches were lighter than others and the client had a fit
[2] the other thing is the plumber Richo has roughed in all the sewer and stormwater under the slab in zone A but theres a problem with the fall on one of the stormwater lines, its only got like 1 in 150 fall and the spec says 1 in 100 minimum, I told him about it and he said he'll fix it this arvo but between you and me I think he knew about it and was hoping nobody would notice, anyway I took a photo of the level on the pipe so we've got a record in case it comes up at inspection
[3] oh and also I nearly forgot the client Sarah came by this morning with her interior designer and they spent about an hour looking at the bathroom tile layout, long story short they want to change the feature wall from the herringbone pattern to a straight stack bond pattern which doesnt really affect us much structurally or anything but it does mean the tiler needs new setting out marks and the waterproofer might need to come back because the niche locations are changing by about 200mm. I said any changes need to be in writing and she said shell email through a variation request by end of day. we'll see.
[4] last thing, the skip bin is overflowing again. I've called the waste company three times this week and they keep saying tomorrow. if they dont pick it up by friday I'm calling someone else. its a OH&S issue having rubbish piling up around the bin. also theres some broken glass near the site entry that needs to be cleaned up before someone drives over it. getting the laborer onto it now.
